faqs.question: What pantry pests are most common in East Meadow homes?
faqs.answer: East Meadow residents most frequently encounter Indian meal moths, flour beetles, sawtoothed grain beetles, and rice weevils. These pests infest dry goods like flour, cereal, pasta, rice, nuts, and dried fruits. They can enter your home through contaminated groceries or find their way through small cracks and gaps around windows and doors.

faqs.question: Do I need to throw away all my pantry food after treatment?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily all items, but our technician will help you identify which products are infested and need disposal. Unopened, sealed packages in unaffected areas are typically safe. We'll inspect your entire pantry, recommend which items to discard, and provide guidance on proper storage methods using airtight containers to prevent re-infestation after treatment.
